description: Lock Fracastoro's contagion theory to medical observation and disease transmission rather than herbal remedy preparation.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to De contagione et contagiosis morbis in 1546.
The corrected medical predecessor is clinical observation and diagnosis.
Clinical observation enables the disease-transmission theory without being a single hard component.
The node remains in Renaissance scholarly and humanist culture.
Herbal preparation should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to remedy making.
